title: Health & Safety Questions (Q&A)
description: Responsibilities, risks, control measures, and safe storage.

questions.prompt: Describe your responsibilities for spotting hazards in the workplace.
questions.exampleContext: Imagine walking past a spill on the workshop floor. Whose responsibility is it to **identify** and **report** this hazard to keep the area **safe**?

questions.prompt: Describe your responsibilities regarding risk assessments.
questions.exampleContext: Before starting a new, potentially dangerous task, you are given a safety document. What are your responsibilities regarding reading and striving to **understand** and **comply** with these **guidelines**?

questions.prompt: Describe your responsibilities regarding COSHH requirements.
questions.exampleContext: You are tasked with cleaning up using industrial solvents. According to COSHH, how should you **handle** and **store** these **chemicals safely**?

questions.prompt: Match the safety concept or risk to its corresponding action or source.

leftItems.text: Using incorrect tool for the job

rightItems.text: Increases risk of slipping and injury
Can damage the workpiece or break the tool

questions.exampleContext: Think about using a hammer with a loose head. Why does using damaged or the **wrong tool** increase the risk of injury or breaking the tool?

questions.prompt: Give an example of when you would use lock off devices.
questions.exampleContext: Imagine you need to repair the internal wiring of a large milling machine. When and why would you **isolate** the **electrical power** using lock off devices?

questions.prompt: Give an example of when you would use fume extraction.
questions.exampleContext: When joining metals with a TIG welder, harmful gases are produced. In what scenarios must you use fume extraction to protect yourself from **dust** or **gas**?

questions.prompt: Explain why tools should be stored in an appropriate manner (e.g. on a tool board).
questions.exampleContext: Imagine needing a specific spanner urgently but the workbench is a mess. Why should tools be organized and stored properly to **prevent loss** or **damage**?

questions.prompt: Why should drawings be returned after use and stored properly?
questions.exampleContext: Engineering drawings are expensive to produce and critical for accuracy. Why must they be kept **clean** and stored **safe**ly for future **reference**?
